title: The `->` operator is the governing mark of every valid path in a Precept definition. You write a `->` for every transition that is allowed; anything not written as an arrow structurally does not exist. The bold solid arrow is the permission system itself — not a diagram element, but the language's declaration that a path is valid.
conceptualBridge: The `->` arrow means every valid state change is an explicitly declared path because anything in a Precept definition without an arrow simply cannot exist — the arrow is the structural permission.
decision: Shortlisted: the `->` operator is the most specific and ownable language element — vivid orange palette new to the set, perfect legibility at any size, highest conceptual bridge score in the language-derived batch.
